text:
RISK BAND

RISK-LEVEL 3 RISK-LEVEL 3

During the month, fund maintained a long-bias with an average unhedged long exposure of 74% and average unhedged short exposure of 9%. Although geopoli Ɵcal volaƟlity conƟnues to remain high, for disciplined investors the vola Ɵlity is viewed as the most signiﬁcant entry point since the pandemic.

The current por ƞolio consists of a mix of large, mid and small caps versus previous month when a large cap skew was maintained, reﬂec Ɵng a mild risk-on environment for India equi Ɵes leading to outperformance by high beta (small & mid cap stocks).

Although ﬁnancial services have the highest weight in the por ƞolio, we are underweight benchmark while having an overweight stance on capital goods and telecom. Fundamental value is o Ōen obscured by emo Ɵonal market extremes. Current c ondi Ɵons are interpreted as a de ﬁni Ɵve signal for conƟnuous aggressive por ƞolio rebalancing.

During the month, the fund maintained an average equity deployment (Long + short) of approximately 90%, average long exposure: ~74%, average short exposure: ~9%, average net exposure: ~65%, month-end por ƞolio beta: 0.54.

qsif Hybrid Long-Short Fund oﬀers a compelling solu Ɵon in the current phase of market turbulence. By integraƟng direc Ɵonal equity exposure with ﬁxed income and yield-generaƟng assets, hybrid long short strategies help cushion por ƞolios against elevated equity vola Ɵlity while enhancing return predictability and downside protec Ɵon. The fund con Ɵnues to maintain an op Ɵmal mix of dir ec Ɵonal equity, arbitrage strategies, InvITs, and ﬁxed income securi Ɵes. This allocaƟon remains dynamic, with conƟnuous rebalancing driven by evolving market c ondi Ɵons, valua Ɵon signals, and risk-reward consideraƟons. Despite geopoli Ɵcal disrup Ɵons, a structural bull run for India is observed globally. A favourable monetary inﬂec Ɵon point has been reached by the naƟon. An aƩr ac Ɵve ﬁxed-income outlook is created by the central bank governor's projec Ɵon of a prolonged low-interest environment. This stability is underpinned by careful ﬁscal Ɵghtening and a nominal GDP growth rate twice that of China. During the month, the fund maintained an average equity deployment (Long + short) of approximately 12%, average long exposure: ~15%, average short exposure: ~9%, average net exposure: ~6%, month-end por ƞolio beta: 0.34.
